question: Identify the root cause of the network fault: which device, what changed, and why it breaks.
description:
R5 (PE-B) changes its Loopback0 prefix-SID from `index 15` to `index 10`
— the same SID already used by R0 (PE-A). Both PEs claim SID index 10
in their LSPs. The SR control plane detects the conflict and rejects
the losing LSP's SR sub-TLV. One PE's loopback becomes unreachable via
SR (only plain IP works). End-to-end SR-TE between PEs breaks.
expected_rca:
Root cause: on 100.64.0.105 (R5, PE-B), the Loopback0 prefix-SID was changed from index 15 to index 10, colliding with R0's existing index 10 (both map to label 16010).
A prefix-SID index must be globally unique within the SR domain; because two loopbacks now claim the same SID label, the SR control plane cannot install both bindings and rejects the losing prefix-SID, so one PE's loopback loses SR reachability (only plain IP works) and end-to-end SR-TE between the PEs breaks.
Fix: restore R5's Loopback0 prefix-SID to index 15.
expected_symptoms_summary:
%ROUTING-ISIS-6-PREFIX_SID_INFO reported by R0, R1, R2 (3 instances):
- R0: ISIS (LAB): Prefix SID info: 'Label Conflict: Prefix-SID label 16010 for algo SPF is used by 192.0.2.10/32 and 192.0.2.15/32'
- R1: ISIS (LAB): Prefix SID info: 'Label Conflict: Prefix-SID label 16010 for algo SPF is used by 192.0.2.10/32 and 192.0.2.15/32'
- R2: ISIS (LAB): Prefix SID info: 'Label Conflict: Prefix-SID label 16010 for algo SPF is used by 192.0.2.10/32 and 192.0.2.15/32'

apply.commands:
configure terminal
router isis LAB
interface Loopback0
 address-family ipv4 unicast
  prefix-sid index 10

revert.commands:
configure terminal
router isis LAB
interface Loopback0
 address-family ipv4 unicast
  prefix-sid index 15
